🎯STRUCK: A Hezbollah command center in Dahieh, Beirut, following their launch of aerial targets toward Israel.

The command center was used by Hezbollah terrorists to advance terrorist attacks against Israeli civilians & IDF soldiers operating in southern Lebanon.

Prior to the strikes, steps were taken to mitigate harm to civilians.

Since the beginning of the year, more than 22,000 pallets of water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) supplies have been approved for the UN agencies and the international organizations.

In practice, however, fewer than 2,000 pallets have actually been coordinated and brought into the Gaza Strip.

Israel continues to allow and facilitate the entry of humanitarian supplies on a large scale. If there is a need for additional equipment and supplies, the approvals are already in place and the organizations have the ability to utilize them.

When less than 10% of the approved supplies are actually coordinated for entry, it is difficult to argue that the problem lies with Israeli restrictions. The approvals exist. What is now required is coordination to turn those approvals into assistance on the ground.

#CoordinationSolvesProblems

Mohammed bin Rashid: I approved the establishment of the Federal Authority for Artificial Intelligence and Data, which will serve as the UAE’s new unified national body for data governance, artificial intelligence, and digital government. The Authority will report to the Cabinet and will be led by Omar Sultan Al Olama.

Our aim is to build a more efficient, agile, and proactive government that leverages technology to better serve people and shape a brighter future for generations to come.

We remain committed to building the government of the future, powered by data and AI-driven tools, enabling faster performance, smarter decision-making, improved public services, and greater opportunities for all.

RT by @DXBMediaOffice: Congratulations to Dubai Basketball on winning the 2025/26 ABA League title following victory over Partizan Belgrade in the fourth game of the final series. This achievement marks a historic milestone just two years after the team’s launch, as Dubai Basketball becomes the first Emirati team to win a major international basketball championship, representing Dubai and the UAE on the global sporting stage. Heartfelt congratulations to the club’s founder and chairman, Abdulla Saeed Al Naboodah, and to all fans, with best wishes for continued success in their upcoming EuroLeague campaign.

شاركت وزارة الداخلية في أعمال القمة العالمية لمكافحة الاحتيال التي استضافتها لشبونة خلال الفترة من 8 إلى 10 يونيو 2026، بصفتها عضواً مؤسساً استراتيجياً في التحالف العالمي لمكافحة الاحتيال (GASA)، وخلال القمة شارك المقدم سعيد خلفان الكعبي مدير إدارة التعاون الدولي،  في اجتماع مجلس إدارة التحالف، حيث جرى بحث التحديات العالمية المرتبطة بجرائم الاحتيال وسبل تعزيز التعاون الدولي لمواجهتها، وأكدت وزارة الداخلية التزام دولة الإمارات بدعم المبادرات الدولية وتطوير الشراكات بين الحكومات وأجهزة إنفاذ القانون والقطاع الخاص، مع التركيز على توظيف التكنولوجيا والذكاء الاصطناعي في الكشف المبكر عن جرائم الاحتيال، وتحليل الأساليب الإجرامية، ورفع كفاءة الاستجابة الأمنية لمواجهة الجرائم العابرة للحدود.

The Ministry of Interior participated in the Global Anti-Scam Summit held in Lisbon, Portugal, from June 8–10, 2026, as a strategic founding member of the Global Anti-Scam Alliance (GASA). Lt. Colonel Saeed Khalfan Al Kaabi, Director of the International Cooperation Department, took part in the Alliance’s Board meeting and summit sessions addressing emerging fraud threats and international cooperation. He reaffirmed the UAE’s commitment to strengthening partnerships and leveraging advanced technologies, including AI, to combat transnational fraud and enhance global security efforts

حقق فريق المشاة بعصا قياس الخطوات المركز الثالث في المنافسات العسكرية الدولية لعام 2026، التي أُقيمت في أكاديمية ساندهيرست الملكية بالمملكة المتحدة، بمشاركة عدد من الفرق العسكرية الدولية.

وتُعد هذه المشاركة الأولى للفريق على المستوى الدولي، حيث نجح في تحقيق إنجاز لافت بحصوله على المركز الثالث، ما يعكس مستوى الكفاءة والجاهزية والاحترافية التي يتمتع بها منتسبوه.

ويأتي هذا الإنجاز ثمرةً للتدريب المتواصل والانضباط العسكري والالتزام بأعلى معايير الأداء، بما يجسد القدرات المتميزة للقوات المسلحة في مختلف المحافل الدولية.

The Infantry Pace Sticking Team secured third place in the pace sticking event at the 2026 International Military Skills Competition, held at the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst in the United Kingdom, with the participation of military teams from several countries.

Marking its first appearance at the international level, the team delivered an impressive performance to claim third place, reflecting the exceptional standards of precision, discipline, readiness and professionalism demonstrated by its members.

This achievement is the culmination of sustained training, rigorous military discipline and adherence to the highest standards of performance, underscoring the distinguished capabilities of the Armed Forces across international forums.

R to @Uaengc: Fortifying National Security Integration, National Guard Commander Visits Dubai Police GHQ to Discuss Joint Cooperation

Major General Saleh Mohammed bin Megren Al Ameri, Commander of the National Guard, visited the Dubai Police GHQ, where he met with H.E. Lieutenant General Abdullah Khalifa Al Marri, Commander in Chief of Dubai Police. The two sides discussed avenues to enhance joint cooperation, coordination, and the exchange of expertise in support of security and stability, and to further strengthen readiness and operational efficiency.

The National Guard Commander affirmed the importance of strategic partnership and continuous cooperation with Dubai Police, noting that both entities operate with a unified team spirit to serve the nation and the community, and to reinforce the state’s security and safety architecture.

For his part, H.E. Lt. Gen. Abdullah Khalifa Al Marri affirmed that the UAE’s operational framework is built on partnership and integration among national entities. He praised the efforts of the National Guard and its vital role in supporting security and stability and emphasized Dubai Police’s commitment to enhancing its operational and institutional readiness through investment in advanced technologies, innovation, and highly qualified personnel.

During the visit, the National Guard Commander was briefed on several specialized facilities and projects within Dubai Police, including the Command and Control Center, the Innovation Lab, the Explosives Security Department, the Resilience Department, and the Drone Systems Center, where he was introduced to the latest technologies and smart systems employed to support security operations and enhance the speed and effectiveness of responding to incident reports.

At the conclusion of the visit, both sides reaffirmed the depth of the institutional relationship and the strategic partnership between the National Guard and Dubai Police, underscoring their commitment to further strengthening cooperation and the exchange of expertise in a manner that serves the national interest and enhances the security and stability of the community.

As part of efforts to strengthen cooperation and exchange expertise, the General Directorate of Drug Enforcement received a delegation of officers from the Malaysian Police. They were welcomed by Colonel Tariq Ali Al Malki, Assistant Director General of Drug Enforcement, along with a number of officers.

During the visit, the Malaysian delegation was briefed on the responsibilities and key functions of the General Directorate of Drug Enforcement, as well as its efforts in combating narcotics. The delegation also learned about the awareness and preventive programs implemented by the Directorate to protect the community.

In addition, the delegation received an overview of the latest methods and technologies used in drug enforcement, as well as the efforts being made to enhance international cooperation in this field.

RT by @mofauae: سعدت بالمشاركة في نقاش مثمر خلال الملتقى العشرين لسفراء الإمارات في العالم، حيث ركز الحوار على التطورات الإقليمية وضرورة الحفاظ على أمن وحرية الملاحة في الممرات المائية الدولية، وفرص المرحلة المقبلة، ودور الدبلوماسية في بناء الشراكات الإستراتيجية. 

شكراً لسمو الشيخ  @ABZayed و@mofauae 🇦🇪 على إتاحة الفرصة للمشاركة في هذا المنتدى الحيوي، والذي أكد أن "المرونة" أصبحت ركيزة أساسية في الاستراتيجيات الوطنية والاقتصادية، وأنها لا تُبنى بجهود فردية، بل عبر بناء شراكات راسخة بين الدول والقطاعات، وهنا يبرز دور الدبلوماسية كمحرك أساسي لضمان تعزيز كل من سلاسل الإمداد والأمن الاقتصادي وأمن الطاقة.

⭕️OPERATIONAL UPDATE: LEBANON
IDF soldiers completed activities to achieve operational control & clear the area north of the Saluki River along the Forward Defense Line.

The Saluki River area has been used by Hezbollah as a prime location for operating explosive drones & launching indirect fire attacks against IDF soldiers.

The troops dismantled hundreds of terrorist structures & eliminated 50+ terrorists. Additionally, several weapons were located, including explosive devices, anti-tank missiles & anti-tank launchers.
